chunks.title: CSS Sizing Units - px, rem, em, vh, vw, % & more
chunks.text: In CSS, you will get to see different types of sizing units.  Sometimes you will get to see pixels, sometimes you will get to see VH,  sometimes you will get to see VW, sometimes you will get to see RAM,  you will get to see M.  Which unit do you want to use? Which is the best among these?

chunks.text: Suppose you have a 1080p monitor,  whose default width is I think 1920x1080.  That is, its height is 1080 and width is 1920.  What will happen in this?  In that, your website code will burst.

chunks.title: CSS Sizing Units - px, rem, em, vh, vw, % & more
chunks.text: So you don't want your website code to burst.  That's why you will use a responsive unit.  So I told you a unit in pixel.  We will not use this.  We will say that its width is 100vw.

chunks.title: CSS Sizing Units - px, rem, em, vh, vw, % & more
chunks.text: Vw means viewport width.  That is, it will take the width of your viewport.  What will happen if I increase it?  Obviously, if I increase it,  then a horizontal scroll bar will come.

chunks.title: CSS Sizing Units - px, rem, em, vh, vw, % & more
chunks.text: You can see.  And let me show you one thing.  As I told you in some of the initial videos,  that you can right click, inspect and click here.  You can click on this TV that you can see here.

chunks.text: Because its width has been measured in the unit of vh.  That's why you didn't see the horizontal scroll bar here.  Okay.  So you can do this thing.  Now if I don't want to use vh.

chunks.title: CSS Sizing Units - px, rem, em, vh, vw, % & more
chunks.text: Margin auto means that I want to automatically apply margin from left and right.  And from margin auto,  your div will automatically apply margin from left and right.  So your div will be centered.  Now look here.

chunks.title: CSS Sizing Units - px, rem, em, vh, vw, % & more
chunks.text: By doing margin auto, it will be centered.  But you have to keep one thing in mind.  If you apply margin auto on it,  it will be centered only when it is a block level element.  If it is an inline element, then it will not be centered.
